mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean excess returns when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances following a >0.2pp UNRATE increase, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered quarters, or the interaction term loses predictive power when GDP growth falls below 1.5% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates into higher corporate profit margins and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns during periods of rising inflation expectations, or the signal Sharpe ratio drops below 0.5 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.4% signals accelerating inflation that forces markets to price in tighter monetary policy, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX yields positive 5-day returns in > 50% of months following CPI spikes above 0.4%, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance in backtesting.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer confidence and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal generates negative 20-day returns in > 55% of observations, or the interaction effect vanishes when wage growth exceeds 5% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts reflect strong residential investment and construction activity, which stimulates upstream supply chains and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST increases fail to predict positive 60-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the signal information coefficient remains below 0.01 across rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% indicates contracting manufacturing output and weakening economic momentum, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of months following sharp INDPRO declines, or the threshold signal loses statistical significance during periods of fiscal stimulus.
